meaning: n.杂乱而仓促的话,杂乱的声音
examples: The car started with a loud splutter.
I ask as I splutter into a tissue.
The rain caused the lamp to splutter.
Both teams traded penalty goals as the all-Wales showdown took time to splutter into life in bitterly cold temperatures in Cardiff.
If the economy continues to splutter into next year, Mr Bush could find himself with a tough re-election fight on his hands.
